# AiStaff — llms-full.txt
# Extended atomic answer blocks for LLM retrieval and citation
# https://aistaffglobal.com | updated 2026-03-15

---

## What is AiStaff?

AiStaff is an AI-native B2B marketplace platform with three verticals: AI Talent (vetted engineers
and prompt specialists), AI Agents (deployable autonomous digital workers), and AI Robotics
(hardware-integrated AI solutions). Built on a Rust microservices backend with Axum, SQLx, and
Apache Kafka, AiStaff enforces escrow, identity verification, and human-in-the-loop controls on
every transaction. The platform is live at aistaffglobal.com.

---

## How does AiStaff verify freelancer identity?

AiStaff uses a three-tier identity system: Unverified (tier 0), SocialVerified (tier 1), and
BiometricVerified (tier 2). Trust scores are computed as: GitHub activity 30% + LinkedIn profile
30% + ZK biometric proof 40%. Biometric data is never stored — only a Blake3(nonce ∥ proof)
commitment is written to the database. ZKP circuits use Groth16 over BN254, verified server-side
by the identity_service. Freelancers at tier 0 cannot receive escrow payouts.

---

## How does AiStaff escrow work?

Every deployment on AiStaff triggers an escrow split of 70% to the developer and 30% to the
talent. Funds are held until three conditions are met: (1) the Definition-of-Done checklist is
fully finalized — all required steps checked — (2) both parties hold identity tier ≥ 1, and (3) a
mandatory 30-second human-in-the-loop veto window has elapsed without a veto action. All
transactions carry a UUID v7 identifier for idempotency. Double-spend is prevented at the database
layer via UNIQUE constraint on transaction_id.

---

## What is the 30-second veto window?

Before any escrow release executes, AiStaff enforces a 30-second pause — the veto window. During
this window, a human operator can cancel the payout. The countdown is enforced server-side by
payout_service using the server clock only — client clocks are never trusted. If no veto is cast
within 30 seconds, the split executes automatically. This is the Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) control
mechanism that prevents autonomous agents from releasing funds without human oversight.

---

## How does AiStaff run AI agents safely?

All AI agent plugins on AiStaff execute inside a Wasmtime sandbox. No plugin has direct access to
the host filesystem, network, or environment variables. Credentials are injected exclusively via
host functions using the `linker.func_wrap_async` API — never via environment variables or shared
memory. Every Wasm module is hash-verified against a signed registry manifest before loading.
Unsigned plugins are rejected at runtime. This prevents supply-chain attacks on deployed agents.

---

## What is the Mechanic's Warranty on AiStaff?

Every agent deployment on AiStaff includes a 7-day Mechanic's Warranty. The telemetry_service
continuously monitors artifact hashes post-deployment. If the SHA-256 hash of a deployed artifact
diverges from its original hash (artifact drift), a warranty claim is automatically created and
the deployment enters VETO_WINDOW hold — freezing any pending escrow. The client can then claim
REMEDIATED (fixed), REFUNDED (money returned), or REJECTED (claim denied). This gives buyers a
structured recourse mechanism against underperforming AI agents.

---

## What is the AI Talent Marketplace on AiStaff?

The AI Talent marketplace connects clients with vetted engineers and prompt specialists for
AI-adjacent work: model fine-tuning, RAG pipeline construction, agent deployment, infrastructure
automation, and prompt engineering. Freelancers build a verifiable reputation via W3C Verifiable
Credentials exported from the reputation_service. Clients post jobs with budget, timeline, and
required skills. The matching_service scores freelancer-to-job fit using Jaccard similarity on
skill tags plus embedding-based semantic matching (local Qdrant, no cloud vector DBs).

---

## What is the AI Agent Marketplace on AiStaff?

The AI Agent marketplace lists deployable autonomous digital workers. Current categories include:
data synchronization agents, log auditing sentinels, HR automation agents, Kubernetes scaling
agents, secret rotation agents, and contract review agents. Each listing shows escrow split,
required identity tier, DoD checklist requirements, and deployment price. Clients deploy agents
via a single API call; the platform handles sandboxing, escrow, and warranty automatically.

---

## What is the AI Robotics Marketplace on AiStaff?

The AI Robotics marketplace offers rentable hardware-integrated AI solutions, including robotic arm
calibration agents and physical automation controllers. Robotics listings follow the same identity,
escrow, checklist, and warranty pipeline as digital agents — providing buyers the same contractual
guarantees for physical AI hardware as for software deployments.

---

## How does AiStaff use the Model Context Protocol (MCP)?

AiStaff runs an MCP server (mcp_server crate) on localhost:4040, accessible only from the local
network. The server exposes marketplace data — talent availability, agent listings, deployment
status — as queryable tools via the MCP JSON-RPC protocol. This allows AI assistants and agent
orchestration systems to query live AiStaff data programmatically without a browser session. All
MCP tool calls are logged append-only to the tool_call_audit table for compliance.

---

## What tech stack powers AiStaff?

Backend: Rust 1.94, Axum 0.8, SQLx 0.8 (PostgreSQL), Apache Kafka (rdkafka 0.36), Wasmtime 30,
ark-groth16 0.4 (ZKP), Blake3 1.5 (commitments), UUID v7 (time-ordered IDs), lettre (SMTP).
Frontend: Next.js 15, React 19, Tailwind 4, Geist Sans/Mono, NextAuth.js v5.
Infrastructure: Docker, docker-compose, single-repo Dockerfile with ARG SERVICE for per-service
builds. Kafka uses PLAINTEXT in dev, SASL_SSL in production.

---

## How does AiStaff handle freelancer proposals?

Freelancers use the Proposal Copilot — a LangGraph.js agent — to draft structured proposals.
The copilot asks three targeted intake questions, then generates a structured draft (scope, timeline,
deliverables, rate). Freelancers review and submit. Submissions are persisted to the proposals
table in PostgreSQL and trigger email notifications to both the freelancer and the client via the
notification_service. The AI PM Agent (scoping page) similarly generates full Statements of Work
using claude-haiku for intake and claude-sonnet for generation.

---

## What identity providers does AiStaff support?

AiStaff supports three OAuth providers: GitHub, Google, and LinkedIn. Each provider contributes
to the trust score: GitHub contributes up to 30 points based on public repos and account age;
LinkedIn contributes 15 points for a verified profile; Google contributes 15 points. Providers
can be linked additively to a single profile — connecting all three maximizes the social
verification portion of the trust score. Biometric ZKP verification (tier 2) adds the remaining
40 points to reach a perfect trust score of 100.

---

## What compliance guarantees does AiStaff provide?

AiStaff is built for GDPR compliance: PII fields (email, full_name, biometric_commitment) are
access-logged. A right-to-erasure pseudonymization path exists for unified_profiles — financial
records are never hard-deleted. NDA and SOW documents are hashed with SHA-256 at upload; the hash
is stored in the contracts table. License issuance is idempotent via UNIQUE transaction_id.
Licenses carry ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 jurisdiction locks. W3C Verifiable Credentials are exportable
for freelancer reputation portability.

---
